page: Amazon Web Services g6.12xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz, delivering 48 dedicated vCPUs across 24 physical cores. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 system memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

For hardware acceleration, the instance bundles four NVIDIA L4 GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, offering a combined 89 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 3760 GB NVMe SSD, and the network interface supports a baseline bandwidth of 40 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance in large language model (LLM) inference tasks. Prompt processing and text generation speeds for small (135M) and medium (7B) models rank in the top 10%, as does prompt processing for large (70B) models. The instance also exhibits strong performance in multi-core CPU execution, ray tracing, image processing, and cryptographic operations, while large-block memory bandwidth and database operations fall within average ranges.

Given the high density of NVIDIA L4 GPUs and dedicated AMD EPYC processing cores, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring parallel graphics processing and machine learning inference without the overhead of larger, ultra-high-end GPU clusters. It is highly suited for LLM inference, ray tracing, image processing, and high-throughput compute tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services g6.12xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance designed for graphics-intensive and machine learning workloads. Powered by the Nitro hypervisor, it features an AMD EPYC 7R13 x86_64 processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs, 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a 3760 GB local NVMe SSD. The hardware configuration includes four NVIDIA L4 Ada Lovelace GPUs with 89 GB of total VRAM, supported by a 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier capabilities in LLM inference, alongside strong multi-core CPU execution, ray tracing, and image processing. This instance is highly suited for machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and compute-heavy workloads requiring local high-speed storage.

meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6.12xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance with 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, four NVIDIA L4 GPUs, and 3760 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated platform featuring four NVIDIA L4 GPUs and local NVMe storage for high-throughput machine learning inference and graphics workloads.
bullet_points: Four NVIDIA L4 Ada Lovelace GPUs with 89 GB total VRAM
48 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs operating at 3.4 GHz
192 GB DDR4 system memory and 3760 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier benchmark performance for LLM inference and prompt processing
40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for machine learning inference, ray tracing, and image processing
